name: Transport & Logistics Compliance Checklist
shortBenefit: Vehicle, driver and freight compliance audit.
intro: Audit your transport and logistics operation against key compliance requirements including vehicle fitness, driver licences, insurance, permits and freight documentation.

seo.title: Transport Compliance Checklist India — Logistics Audit | Varada Nexus
seo.description: Free transport compliance checklist India. Audit vehicle fitness, driver licences, insurance, permits and freight documentation for logistics operations.

items.text: Registration Certificate (RC) valid and not expired
Fitness Certificate (FC) valid
Motor Vehicles Insurance (Third Party + Own Damage)
National Permit / State Permit valid
PUC (Pollution Under Control) certificate valid
Commercial Driving Licence (CDL) valid for vehicle class
Driver medical fitness certificate current
Driver rest hours compliant (max 8h driving, rest required)
Speed governor / vehicle tracking unit (VTU) fitted and active
E-way bill generated for goods ≥₹50,000 value (GST)
Lorry Receipt (LR) / consignment note issued
Goods and Services Tax (GST) documents in order
Dangerous goods declaration (if applicable)
Load within permissible GVW limit
Cargo properly secured to prevent shifting
Hazardous cargo labelled per HMRT rules (if applicable)
Oversized / ODC cargo with special permit (if applicable)
First aid kit, fire extinguisher and reflective triangles in vehicle
Fleet maintenance records up to date
GPS/vehicle tracking active and reporting
Driver training records maintained

assumptions: Based on Motor Vehicles Act 1988, Central Motor Vehicles Rules and GST E-way bill requirements. State-specific rules may add requirements.
disclaimer: This tool provides an indicative estimate for general planning only and is not professional, legal, financial, medical or engineering advice. Reference values are editable defaults that vary by location, vendor, regulation and date. Verify critical figures with a qualified professional. Varada Nexus accepts no liability for decisions made using this tool.

faq.a: An E-way bill is an electronic document generated on the GST portal for movement of goods worth more than ₹50,000. It is mandatory for interstate transport and for intrastate movement in most states.

faq.q: What permits does a truck need for interstate transport?
faq.a: For interstate goods transport: National Permit from the MV Department, RC, FC, insurance, PUC and driver's commercial licence. Goods requiring special handling (chemicals, food, pharma) may need additional permits.

faq.q: What are the penalties for driving without a fitness certificate?
faq.a: Driving without a valid FC attracts a fine and vehicle detention. For commercial vehicles, the fine can be up to ₹10,000. Repeat offences can lead to suspension of the vehicle permit.
